title: vernonia greggii subsp. ervendbergii
intro: Vernonia greggii subsp. ervendbergii, also known as Mexican ironweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to Mexico. It is a shrub-like plant with small purple flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Vernonia greggii subsp. ervendbergii include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
